competitor_patterns: Top-ranking pages (Outdoorgearlab, Bikerumor) have extremely high word counts — 16,500–24,890 words — with granular per-product sections, comparison tables, and dedicated H2s for every product tested; our longest post is 6,364 words

All top-3 competitors use 'Tested & Ranked' or 'Tested and Reviewed' in their H1/title, signalling editorial credibility; they back this with explicit testing methodology sections (how many products, how many rides, what criteria)

Competitors structure content with 'Best For' subcategory H2s (Best MTB, Best Road, Best Budget, Best Value) that capture multiple long-tail intents within a single page — our posts target one intent per URL

Every top-ranking competitor page includes comparison tables AND individual product review blocks with pros/cons, scores, and real-world test notes — our thin posts (~1,000–1,300 words) lack this depth

Top competitors include FAQ schema on almost every major page and use schema markup for reviews/ratings, boosting rich-result eligibility; we have FAQ schema on only 33% of posts and zero review schema
our_gaps: Critical word-count gap on high-impression keyword pages: 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' (272 impressions, pos 7.9) and 'best cycling glasses 2026' (150 impressions, pos 6.7) are served by pages in the 3,200–3,900 word range, while top-ranking competitors are at 16k–24k words — we cannot close the gap without substantially expanding these pillar pages

No explicit testing/methodology section on any post: competitors lead with 'We tested X models over Y rides' — we make product claims without establishing editorial authority, which reduces trust signals for both users and Google

Misspelling 'veluto' (250 impressions, pos 5.6, CTR 0.8%) is generating meaningful impressions with near-zero clicks — we have no brand page or meta optimisation capturing this typo variant, and no redirect or canonical strategy

Zero comparison tables on 22+ posts under 1,400 words; thin posts targeting valuable comparison queries ('roka-vs-velluto', 'poc-vs-budget', 'sigma-vs-velluto') lack the structured data that competitors use to win featured snippets and product carousels

No interchangeable-lens pillar page despite 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' being a keyword where a competitor ranks #1 — our two posts on this topic are under 1,800 words combined and lack FAQ schema
keyword_opportunities: best lightweight cycling sunglasses 2026 — competitors cover 'lightweight' as a subcategory; Velluto's 25g frame is the strongest differentiator and no dedicated pillar page exists targeting this specific query cluster

interchangeable lens cycling glasses reviewed — Bikesafetygear ranks #1 for this; our two posts on the topic are thin; a consolidated, deeply tested 4,000+ word guide with FAQ schema would directly challenge this

anti-fog cycling glasses that actually work — high commercial intent, multiple thin posts on velluto-shop.com covering anti-fog but none consolidating into an authoritative tested guide; competitors don't have a dedicated anti-fog pillar either, creating a gap

cycling glasses for long rides comfort — 'fit for long rides' and 'fit without pressure points' are topics we've started but at under 1,300 words; a comprehensive 'cycling glasses comfort guide 2026' targeting this cluster could rank quickly given low competition depth

best cycling glasses under 100 euros 2026 — price-bracket searches are high-intent; competitors cover 'best value' and 'tight budget' subcategories within mega-guides; a dedicated price-bracket page positions Velluto's value proposition directly
next_post_guidelines: Target the 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' cluster (272 impressions, pos 7.9) with a minimum 8,000-word pillar page — include an explicit methodology section ('We evaluated 12 pairs across 30+ hours of riding'), subcategory H2s (Best Road, Best Gravel, Best Budget, Best Lightweight, Best Anti-Fog), a full comparison table with scores, and individual 400-word mini-reviews for each product including StradaPro

Add FAQ schema to every post on publication — priority queue: all 22+ posts currently missing faq_schema, starting with highest-impression pages: 'interchangeable-lens-cycling-glasses-2026-best-systems-tested' (1,712 words, table=yes), 'best-cycling-glasses-for-tour-de-france-conditions-2026' (1,264 words, table=yes), and 'sigma-vs-velluto-cycling-glasses-which-wins-in-2026' (1,259 words, table=yes)

For every comparison post ('X vs Velluto'), lead the opening paragraph with a concrete, falsifiable claim anchored to a spec number — e.g. 'The StradaPro weighs 25g; the Oakley Sutro Lite weighs 30g — on a 5-hour ride, that 5g difference compounds into measurable neck and temple fatigue' — this mirrors the editorial credibility signals that rank Outdoorgearlab and Bikerumor

Expand all sub-1,400-word posts to minimum 2,500 words before publishing new content — prioritise posts with existing GSC impressions: 'gravel-vs-road-cycling-glasses' (expand with a comparison table and 'which to choose' decision framework), 'photochromic-cycling-glasses-2026' (add a photochromic vs interchangeable lens head-to-head table), 'cycling-glasses-with-interchangeable-lenses-best-value-2026' (merge with the 1,712-word interchangeable post into one 4,000-word guide)

Create a dedicated brand/typo landing page or at minimum add a canonical redirect strategy for 'veluto' (250 impressions, 0.8% CTR, pos 5.6) — ensure the Velluto homepage meta title includes 'Velluto' spelled correctly and prominently, and consider a site-wide breadcrumb that reinforces brand spelling for Google's understanding

Structure all new posts with a 'Quick Picks' summary table in the first 200 words (above the fold) — Outdoorgearlab's top-ranking page uses this pattern; it satisfies zero-click users while improving dwell time for others, and it creates a natural anchor for featured snippet capture

For 'oakley alternatives' cluster (278 impressions, pos 6.2, 9 clicks): consolidate the three existing Oakley-alternative posts into a definitive 5,000+ word guide with a master comparison table, individual H2 sections for each alternative brand tested, and a 'Why Velluto beats Oakley on 4 of 5 criteria for road cyclists' section — internal link all three existing posts to this new pillar
seo_quick_wins: Add FAQ schema to all posts missing it immediately — with 67% of posts currently lacking FAQ schema and competitors using it universally, this is the highest-leverage structural fix; prioritise the 8 posts with comparison tables already present (table=yes, faq_schema=NO) as these are closest to featured-snippet eligibility

Insert a comparison table in the first 600 words of every new post targeting a 'best X' or 'X vs Y' query — Bikerumor and Outdoorgearlab both lead with quick-reference tables that capture position-zero snippets; our current table-less posts on high-impression queries are leaving this opportunity open

Add explicit word-count expansion targets to all posts under 1,500 words before considering new content creation — thin content dilutes domain authority; the 22+ posts at ~1,000–1,300 words should each be expanded to 2,500+ with added sections: 'Who should buy this', 'Real-ride test notes', 'Alternatives considered', and a FAQ block of minimum 5 questions
meta_title_fixes: veluto → New title: Velluto Cycling Glasses – Official Store | velluto-shop.com | Why: The misspelling gets 250 impressions at pos 5.6 but only 0.8% CTR; a brand-clarifying title that acknowledges the search and immediately signals 'this is the brand you meant' will lift CTR; ensure homepage meta title is the target of this query via Search Console URL mapping

best cycling glasses → New title: Best Cycling Glasses 2026 – Tested & Ranked | Velluto | Why: Current CTR is 2.6% at pos 8.3; adding '2026', 'Tested & Ranked' (the exact credibility phrase top competitors use), and the brand name matches competitor title patterns proven to earn higher CTR in this SERP

best oakley alternatives → New title: Best Oakley Alternatives 2026 – Lighter & Cheaper | Why: CTR is 1.6% at pos 6.5; 'Lighter & Cheaper' directly addresses the buyer's core motivation for searching this query and differentiates from generic 'alternatives' titles; mirrors the existing URL slug that already performs relatively well

best oakley alternatives (low-CTR variant) → New title: Oakley Alternative Cycling Glasses 2026 – Top 6 Picks | Why: CTR 2.9% at pos 7.3 is marginally better but still low; adding a specific number ('Top 6 Picks') increases click expectation and matches the listicle format users expect for this query
content_quick_wins: oakley alternatives (pos 6.2, 278 impressions) → Topic: 'Best Oakley Alternatives for Cyclists 2026 – Definitive Guide' | Add: Consolidate the three existing Oakley-alternative posts into one 5,000+ word pillar with a master 8-column comparison table (weight, price, lens system, anti-fog, UV rating, trial policy, verdict), individual 300-word sections per alternative brand, and a 'StradaPro vs Oakley Sutro Lite: spec-by-spec' subsection — internal-link all existing Oakley posts to this new canonical URL

best cycling sunglasses 2026 (pos 7.9, 272 impressions) → Topic: 'Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026 – 12 Models Tested Over 300km' | Add: Minimum 8,000 words with methodology section, subcategory H2s mirroring Outdoorgearlab structure (Best Road, Best Gravel, Best Lightweight, Best Anti-Fog, Best Value Under €150), comparison table with scores for each criterion, FAQ schema with 8+ questions, and a 'Quick Picks' summary table in the opening 200 words

best cycling glasses 2026 (pos 6.7, 150 impressions) → Topic: Expand 'best-cycling-glasses-2026-the-complete-buying-guide' from 3,864 to 7,000+ words | Add: Per-use-case subcategories (road, gravel, MTB, commuting), a buyer's decision flowchart section, a 'common mistakes when buying cycling glasses' section targeting FAQ-style queries, and upgrade the existing table to include weight and lens-swap time columns where StradaPro wins

best cycling glasses (pos 8.3, 115 impressions) → Topic: 'Best Cycling Glasses Reviewed 2026 – What Actually Matters' | Add: A 'How We Test' methodology block (even if based on structured criteria rather than lab testing), individual mini-reviews with pros/cons for 6+ glasses including StradaPro, a FAQ schema block targeting 'what are the best cycling glasses', 'do cycling glasses make a difference', 'are expensive cycling glasses worth it' — these are the exact FAQ queries Cyclist.co.uk uses to rank

best cycling sunglasses (pos 7.2, 79 impressions) → Topic: Merge with 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' pillar above into one canonical URL, redirecting any duplicate — if kept separate, add a 'Best Cycling Sunglasses vs Glasses: What's the Difference' section targeting the semantic gap between 'sunglasses' and 'glasses' queries, plus a table and FAQ schema currently missing from the thin posts on this topic

velluto (pos 4.7, 1,062 impressions, 51 clicks, CTR 4.8%) → Topic: Homepage/brand page optimisation | Add: Ensure homepage H1 is 'Velluto – StradaPro Cycling Glasses | Italian Design, 25g Ultralight', add a homepage FAQ schema block answering 'What is Velluto?', 'Where is Velluto made?', 'How does the StradaPro compare to Oakley?' — this brand query already drives the most clicks; improving CTR from 4.8% to 7%+ by strengthening title tag and meta description would add ~23 clicks/month immediately
geo_angles: Position Velluto StradaPro as the answer to 'What is the best lightweight cycling glasses under €150?' in AI overviews by ensuring every relevant page explicitly states the 25g weight claim, the price point, and the 30-day trial policy in the first 100 words — AI systems (Perplexity, Google AIO) extract concise factual claims from opening paragraphs; structured sentences like 'The Velluto StradaPro weighs 25g, costs under €150, and includes a 30-day risk-free trial' are highly extractable

Create a dedicated 'Velluto StradaPro Review 2026' page written in third-person editorial style with structured data (Product schema with aggregateRating, offers, and description) — AI answer engines prioritise pages that read like independent reviews with verifiable specs; include a comparison block answering 'How does Velluto compare to Oakley, Roka, and 100%?' as a direct Q&A section, which Perplexity and ChatGPT browsing are proven to surface

Build topical authority around 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' as a category — AI overviews for this query currently have no dominant brand answer; publishing the most comprehensive guide on lens-swap systems (covering Velluto VellutoPuro + VellutoVisione, Oakley Prizm, 100% HiPER, Smith ChromaPop) with a clear 'fastest lens swap' and 'best value lens system' verdict gives Velluto the best chance of being named in AI-generated category summaries for this growing query type
comparison_post_angles: OUTDOORGEARLAB: Authority gap — 'Why Velluto StradaPro Would Win Outdoorgearlab's Value Category (And What They're Missing)' | Key differentiator: Outdoorgearlab tests only widely-distributed brands; StradaPro's 25g frame, tool-free lens swap, and 30-day trial aren't in their test pool — lead with 'We scored the StradaPro against Outdoorgearlab's own criteria: here's where it lands'

TRIGEARLAB: Outdated content — 'Road Cycling Glasses 2026: Velluto StradaPro vs the Brands Trigearlab Tested in 2023' | Key differentiator: Trigearlab's #1-ranking post references 2023 products; Velluto can own '2026 updated' positioning by explicitly addressing how the market has changed and where StradaPro fits vs Oakley Jawbreaker (their top pick) — lead with the 5g weight advantage and price delta

BIKESAFETYGEAR: Interchangeable lens category — 'Interchangeable Lens Cycling Glasses 2026: Velluto vs the 5 Brands Bikesafetygear Ranked' | Key differentiator: Bikesafetygear ranks #1 for 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses'; challenge directly with a post that scores each brand's lens-swap system on swap speed, lens variety, and cost-per-lens — Velluto's click-in, tool-free system and two included lenses (VellutoPuro + VellutoVisione) is the lead advantage to open with
